teacher_output:
Looking at your Trader Joe's receipt, I can see some great ingredients for cooking! Here are my recipe suggestions:

RECIPE 1: Mediterranean Veggie Scramble
USES: eggs, red bell pepper, tomatoes, mozzarella cheese, avocado
TIME: 15 minutes
DIFFICULTY: easy
STEPS:
1. Dice the red bell pepper and tomatoes
2. Heat a non-stick pan over medium heat
3. Sauté the diced bell pepper for 3-4 minutes until softened
4. Beat the eggs and pour into the pan with peppers
5. Add diced tomatoes and scramble everything together
6. Top with shredded mozzarella and let it melt
7. Serve with sliced avocado on the side
8. Season with salt and pepper to taste

RECIPE 2: Fresh Garden Salad with Peanut Dressing
USES: carrots, cucumbers, tomatoes, avocado, peanut butter
TIME: 10 minutes
DIFFICULTY: easy
STEPS:
1. Slice the cucumbers and dice the tomatoes
2. Use the pre-shredded carrots as your base
3. Combine vegetables in a large bowl
4. Make dressing by mixing 2 tbsp peanut butter with 2 tbsp warm water
5. Add a pinch of salt to the dressing and whisk until smooth
6. Toss salad with the peanut dressing
7. Top with sliced avocado just before serving

RECIPE 3: Hearty Oatmeal Breakfast Bowl
USES: oatmeal, bananas, peanut butter
TIME: 8 minutes
DIFFICULTY: easy
STEPS:
1. Cook the oatmeal according to package directions with water
2. Slice the bananas while oatmeal cooks
3. Once oatmeal is ready, stir in 1 tbsp peanut butter
4. Top with sliced bananas
5. Add a pinch of salt to enhance flavors
6. Serve warm for a filling breakfast

Looking at your SPAR receipt, I can see some great ingredients to work with! Here are my recipe suggestions:

RECIPE 1: Smoked Vienna and Peach Rice Bowl
USES: smoked viennas, peeled peaches, jasmine rice, mediterranean mix
TIME: 25 minutes
DIFFICULTY: easy
STEPS:
1. Cook jasmine rice according to package directions with salt and water
2. Slice smoked viennas into rounds and pan-fry until golden
3. Drain and chop peeled peaches into chunks
4. Heat mediterranean mix herbs with a splash of water to rehydrate
5. Serve rice topped with viennas, peaches, and seasoned with mediterranean herbs
6. Add salt and pepper to taste

RECIPE 2: English Cucumber and Grape Salad with Bananas
USES: english cucumber, grapes mixed, bananas loose
TIME: 15 minutes
DIFFICULTY: easy
STEPS:
1. Wash and slice english cucumber into rounds
2. Wash grapes and remove from stems
3. Peel and slice bananas
4. Combine all fruits in a large bowl
5. Season lightly with salt and pepper
6. Toss gently and let flavors meld for 10 minutes
7. Serve chilled as a refreshing side dish

RECIPE 3: Hearty Vienna and Vegetable Rice Skillet
USES: smoked viennas, jasmine rice, english cucumber, mediterranean mix, cooking oil
TIME: 30 minutes
DIFFICULTY: medium
STEPS:
1. Heat cooking oil in a large skillet over medium heat
2. Cook jasmine rice with water and salt until tender
3. Slice smoked viennas and cucumber
4. Sauté viennas in the skillet until browned
5. Add cooked rice and diced cucumber to skillet
6. Season with rehydrated mediterranean mix herbs
7. Stir-fry everything together for 5-7 minutes until heated through
8. Adjust seasoning with salt and pepper

Looking at your Whole Foods receipt, I can see some great ingredients to work with! Here are my recipe suggestions:

RECIPE 1: Strawberry Cottage Cheese Bowl with Mahi Mahi
USES: whole strawberries, cottage cheese, mahi mahi fillets
TIME: 20 minutes
DIFFICULTY: easy
STEPS:
1. Season mahi mahi fillets with salt and pepper
2. Heat a pan with a little oil over medium-high heat
3. Cook mahi mahi for 4-5 minutes per side until flaky
4. Meanwhile, wash and slice fresh strawberries
5. Place cottage cheese in a bowl and top with sliced strawberries
6. Serve the grilled mahi mahi alongside the cottage cheese bowl
7. Drizzle with a touch of water mixed with salt for extra flavor

RECIPE 2: Black Bean and Mango Quesadillas
USES: tortillas, black beans, frozen mangoes
TIME: 15 minutes
DIFFICULTY: easy
STEPS:
1. Thaw frozen mango pieces and dice them
2. Drain and rinse black beans, then mash half of them lightly
3. Heat tortillas in a dry pan for 30 seconds to soften
4. Spread mashed and whole black beans on half of each tortilla
5. Add diced mango pieces on top of beans
6. Fold tortillas in half and cook in pan 2-3 minutes per side
7. Cut into wedges and serve hot

RECIPE 3: Hickory-Crusted Mahi with Apple Plum Salsa
USES: mahi mahi fillets, gala apples, black plums, hickory (as seasoning reference)
TIME: 25 minutes
DIFFICULTY: medium
STEPS:
1. Dice gala apples and black plums into small cubes
2. Mix diced fruit with salt and pepper to make a fresh salsa
3. Season mahi mahi fillets generously with salt and pepper
4. Heat pan over medium-high heat with a little water
5. Cook mahi mahi 5-6 minutes per side until golden
6. Let fish rest for 2 minutes after cooking
7. Serve mahi mahi topped with the fresh apple-plum salsa

Looking at your Walmart receipt, I can see you purchased:
- Bananas (0.41 lb)
- FRAP (appears to be a Frappuccino or similar beverage)

Since there are only 2 items and one is a beverage, I'll focus on creative ways to use the bananas along with basic pantry staples:

RECIPE 1: Simple Banana Pancakes
USES: bananas
TIME: 15 minutes
DIFFICULTY: easy
STEPS:
1. Mash the bananas in a bowl until smooth
2. Add 2 eggs and mix well
3. Add 2 tablespoons flour and a pinch of salt
4. Heat a non-stick pan over medium heat
5. Pour small amounts of batter to form pancakes
6. Cook 2-3 minutes per side until golden
7. Serve warm

RECIPE 2: Caramelized Banana Toast
USES: bananas
TIME: 10 minutes
DIFFICULTY: easy
STEPS:
1. Slice bananas into rounds
2. Heat 1 tablespoon butter in a pan over medium heat
3. Add banana slices and cook 2 minutes
4. Sprinkle with a little sugar or honey if available
5. Cook another 2 minutes until caramelized
6. Toast bread slices
7. Top toast with caramelized bananas
8. Sprinkle with cinnamon if available

RECIPE 3: Banana "Ice Cream"
USES: bananas
TIME: 5 minutes prep + 2 hours freezing
DIFFICULTY: easy
STEPS:
1. Slice bananas and freeze for at least 2 hours
2. Remove frozen banana slices from freezer
3. Blend or mash until creamy and smooth
4. Add a splash of water if needed for consistency
5. Serve immediately for soft-serve texture
6. Or refreeze for 30 minutes for firmer texture

Here are some practical recipes using your ingredients:

RECIPE 1: Ramen Breakfast Bowl
USES: Ramen noodles, eggs
TIME: 10 minutes
DIFFICULTY: easy
STEPS:
1. Boil water in a pot and cook ramen noodles for 2 minutes (discard seasoning packet or save for later)
2. Crack 1-2 eggs directly into the boiling ramen water
3. Gently stir to break up egg whites and create ribbons
4. Cook for 2-3 more minutes until eggs are cooked through
5. Season with salt and pepper to taste
6. Serve hot in a bowl

RECIPE 2: English Muffin Egg Sandwich
USES: English muffins, eggs
TIME: 8 minutes
DIFFICULTY: easy
STEPS:
1. Split and toast English muffins until golden brown
2. Heat a non-stick pan over medium heat
3. Crack eggs into pan and cook sunny-side up or scrambled as preferred
4. Season eggs with salt and pepper
5. Place cooked eggs between toasted English muffin halves
6. Serve immediately while warm

RECIPE 3: Loaded Ramen with Poached Egg
USES: Ramen noodles, eggs
TIME: 12 minutes
DIFFICULTY: medium
STEPS:
1. Bring water to boil in a small pot for poaching egg
2. In another pot, cook ramen noodles with half the seasoning packet
3. Crack egg into a small bowl, then gently slide into boiling water
4. Poach egg for 3-4 minutes until whites are set
5. Drain ramen and place in serving bowl
6. Top with poached egg and season with pepper
7. Break yolk and mix through noodles before eating

Looking at your Walmart receipt, I can identify these food items: bananas, beverage, cranberry pomegranate juice, strawberry, campari tomatoes, avocado, and hard salami.

RECIPE 1: Fresh Fruit & Salami Breakfast Bowl
USES: bananas, strawberry, hard salami, avocado
TIME: 10 minutes
DIFFICULTY: easy
STEPS:
1. Slice bananas into rounds and dice strawberries
2. Cut avocado in half, remove pit, and slice into wedges
3. Dice hard salami into small cubes
4. Arrange fruit in a bowl with avocado slices on the side
5. Top with salami cubes for protein
6. Add a pinch of salt and pepper to the avocado
7. Serve immediately for a balanced breakfast

RECIPE 2: Savory Tomato Avocado Salad with Salami
USES: campari tomatoes, avocado, hard salami
TIME: 15 minutes
DIFFICULTY: easy
STEPS:
1. Wash and slice campari tomatoes into rounds
2. Cut avocado in half, remove pit, and cube the flesh
3. Dice hard salami into bite-sized pieces
4. Combine tomatoes, avocado, and salami in a large bowl
5. Season with salt, pepper, and a splash of water if needed
6. Toss gently to combine without mashing the avocado
7. Let sit for 5 minutes to allow flavors to meld
8. Serve as a refreshing lunch or side dish

RECIPE 3: Tropical Smoothie Bowl
USES: bananas, strawberry, cranberry pomegranate juice
TIME: 8 minutes
DIFFICULTY: easy
STEPS:
1. Peel and freeze banana slices for 10 minutes if you have time
2. Hull and quarter the strawberries
3. Add bananas, strawberries, and 1/2 cup cranberry pomegranate juice to blender
4. Blend until smooth and thick
5. Pour into a bowl
6. Slice remaining fruit for toppings if desired
7. Serve immediately with a spoon

Looking at your Walmart receipt, I can identify these ingredients: water, wheat (flour), meat (appears to be chicken), vegetable oil, cherries, eggs, and what appears to be some pantry items. Here are 3 practical recipes:

RECIPE 1: Pan-Fried Chicken with Cherry Glaze
USES: chicken, cherries, vegetable oil, water
TIME: 25 minutes
DIFFICULTY: medium
STEPS:
1. Season chicken pieces with salt and pepper
2. Heat vegetable oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat
3. Cook chicken 6-7 minutes per side until golden and cooked through
4. Remove chicken and set aside
5. Add cherries and 2 tablespoons water to same pan
6. Simmer 3-4 minutes, mashing cherries slightly to create a glaze
7. Return chicken to pan and coat with cherry glaze
8. Serve immediately

RECIPE 2: Simple Cherry Pancakes
USES: wheat flour, eggs, water, cherries, vegetable oil
TIME: 20 minutes
DIFFICULTY: easy
STEPS:
1. Mix 1 cup flour with pinch of salt in a bowl
2. Whisk together 1 egg and 3/4 cup water
3. Combine wet and dry ingredients until just mixed
4. Chop cherries into small pieces
5. Heat oil in a non-stick pan over medium heat
6. Pour batter to make pancakes, sprinkle cherries on top
7. Cook 2-3 minutes per side until golden
8. Serve hot

RECIPE 3: Basic Fried Chicken Bites
USES: chicken, wheat flour, eggs, vegetable oil, water
TIME: 30 minutes
DIFFICULTY: medium
STEPS:
1. Cut chicken into bite-sized pieces
2. Set up breading station: flour in one bowl, beaten egg in another
3. Season flour with salt and pepper
4. Dredge chicken in flour, then egg, then flour again
5. Heat oil in deep pan to 350°F
6. Fry chicken pieces 4-5 minutes until golden and cooked through
7. Drain on paper towels
8. Season with salt while hot

Looking at your Walmart receipt, I can see you purchased: grill cover, beef, celery heart, and red grapes. Here are some practical recipes using these ingredients:

RECIPE 1: Pan-Seared Beef with Celery
USES: beef, celery heart
TIME: 25 minutes
DIFFICULTY: easy
STEPS:
1. Remove beef from refrigerator 15 minutes before cooking to reach room temperature
2. Season beef generously with salt and pepper on both sides
3. Chop celery heart into small pieces
4. Heat oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat
5. Sear beef for 3-4 minutes per side for medium-rare, adjust for desired doneness
6. Remove beef and let rest, sauté celery in same pan for 3-4 minutes
7. Slice beef and serve topped with sautéed celery

RECIPE 2: Beef and Grape Skillet
USES: beef, red grapes, celery heart
TIME: 30 minutes
DIFFICULTY: medium
STEPS:
1. Cut beef into bite-sized strips and season with salt and pepper
2. Dice celery heart and halve the red grapes
3. Heat oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat
4. Cook beef strips for 4-5 minutes until browned
5. Add celery and cook for 3 minutes until softened
6. Add grapes and cook 2-3 minutes until they start to release juices
7. Add a splash of water if needed, simmer 2 minutes and serve hot

RECIPE 3: Simple Beef and Celery Stir-Fry
USES: beef, celery heart
TIME: 20 minutes
DIFFICULTY: easy
STEPS:
1. Slice beef thinly against the grain
2. Cut celery heart into diagonal slices
3. Heat oil in a wok or large skillet over high heat
4. Add beef and stir-fry for 3-4 minutes until browned
5. Add celery and stir-fry for 2-3 minutes until crisp-tender
6. Season with salt and pepper to taste
7. Serve immediately over rice if available

Looking at your Whole Foods receipt, I can see you have kettle-cooked sea salt potato chips, braided brioche, and two chef plate meals. Since the chef plate meals are already prepared, I'll focus on creative ways to use your other items plus basic pantry staples.

RECIPE 1: Brioche French Toast with Crushed Chip Coating
USES: braided brioche, sea salt potato chips
TIME: 15 minutes
DIFFICULTY: easy
STEPS:
1. Slice the braided brioche into thick pieces (about 1 inch)
2. Crush the sea salt potato chips into coarse crumbs in a shallow dish
3. Whisk together 2 eggs with a splash of water in another shallow dish
4. Dip each brioche slice in egg mixture, then coat both sides with crushed chips
5. Heat a pan over medium heat and cook each slice for 2-3 minutes per side until golden
6. Serve immediately while the chip coating is still crispy

RECIPE 2: Savory Brioche Bread Pudding
USES: braided brioche, sea salt potato chips
TIME: 45 minutes
DIFFICULTY: medium
STEPS:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F and tear brioche into bite-sized pieces
2. Place brioche pieces in a greased baking dish
3. Whisk together 3 eggs, 1 cup water, salt and pepper
4. Pour egg mixture over brioche and let soak for 10 minutes
5. Crush half the potato chips and sprinkle on top for texture
6. Bake for 25-30 minutes until set and golden on top
